questions.q: What are vectors? Explain the basic properties of vector addition and scalar multiplication. Also, prove that the vector sum of three vectors is independent of the order in which they are added.
questions.answer: Vectors are quantities with both magnitude and direction. Vector addition and scalar multiplication have several properties, including commutativity, associativity, and distributivity. To prove that the vector sum of three vectors is independent of the order in which they are added, we can use the associative property of vector addition: (a + b) + c = a + (b + c).
marking_scheme: Definition of vectors (1 mark)
Explanation of vector addition properties (1 mark)
Explanation of scalar multiplication properties (1 mark)
Proof of vector sum independence (2 marks)

questions.q: Find the unit vector in the direction of the vector 2i + 3j - k. Also, find the vector in the direction of 3i - 4j + 2k and having magnitude 5.
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
To find the unit vector, first, find the magnitude of the vector: |a| = sqrt(2^2 + 3^2 + (-1)^2) = sqrt(4 + 9 + 1) = sqrt(14).
Then, the unit vector is a/|a| = (2i + 3j - k)/sqrt(14).
For the second part, the unit vector in the direction of 3i - 4j + 2k is (3i - 4j + 2k)/sqrt(3^2 + (-4)^2 + 2^2) = (3i - 4j + 2k)/sqrt(9 + 16 + 4) = (3i - 4j + 2k)/sqrt(29).
The vector with magnitude 5 in this direction is 5 * (3i - 4j + 2k)/sqrt(29).
marking_scheme: Finding magnitude of the first vector (1 mark)
Finding unit vector in the direction of the first vector (1 mark)
Finding unit vector in the direction of the second vector (1 mark)
Finding vector with specified magnitude in the direction of the second vector (2 marks)

questions.q: What is the difference between a scalar triple product and a vector triple product? Derive the formula for the scalar triple product and explain its geometric interpretation.
questions.answer: The scalar triple product is a scalar value, while the vector triple product is a vector. The formula for the scalar triple product of three vectors a, b, and c is a . (b x c), which can be derived using the properties of the dot product and the cross product. Geometrically, it represents the volume of the parallelepiped with adjacent sides a, b, and c.
marking_scheme: Definition of scalar and vector triple products (1 mark)
Derivation of the scalar triple product formula (2 marks)
Geometric interpretation of the scalar triple product (2 marks)

questions.q: If a = i + j + k, b = 2i - j + 3k, and c = -i + 2j - 4k, find (a x b) . c and a . (b x c).
marking_scheme: Finding a x b (2 marks)
Finding (a x b) . c (1 mark)
Finding b x c (2 marks)
Finding a . (b x c) (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the concept of collinearity of vectors. Prove that three vectors a, b, and c are coplanar if and only if the scalar triple product [a b c] is zero.
questions.answer: Three vectors are said to be coplanar if they lie in the same plane. The scalar triple product [a b c] = a . (b x c) gives the volume of the parallelepiped formed by these vectors. If the volume is zero, it means the vectors are coplanar. To prove that [a b c] = 0 implies coplanarity, we can show that if a, b, and c are coplanar, then a can be expressed as a linear combination of b and c, which leads to [a b c] = 0.
marking_scheme: Definition of coplanarity (1 mark)
Explanation of the scalar triple product (1 mark)
Proof of the condition for coplanarity (3 marks)

questions.q: Given two vectors a = 2i - j + k and b = -i + 2j - 3k, find the projection of a on b and the projection of b on a. Also, find the angle between the two vectors.
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
First, find the dot product a . b = (2i - j + k) . (-i + 2j - 3k)
= -2 - 2 - 3
= -7.
Next, find the magnitude of b: |b| = sqrt((-1)^2 + 2^2 + (-3)^2) = sqrt(1 + 4 + 9) = sqrt(14).
Then, the projection of a on b is (a . b)/|b|^2 * b = (-7)/(14) * (-i + 2j - 3k) = (i - 2j + 3k)/2.
Similarly, find the magnitude of a: |a| = sqrt(2^2 + (-1)^2 + 1^2) = sqrt(4 + 1 + 1) = sqrt(6).
Then, the projection of b on a is (a . b)/|a|^2 * a = (-7)/(6) * (2i - j + k) = (-7/3)i + (7/6)j - (7/6)k.
To find the angle theta between a and b, use the formula cos(theta) = (a . b)/( |a| * |b| ) = -7/(sqrt(6) * sqrt(14)).
marking_scheme: Finding dot product a . b (1 mark)
Finding projection of a on b (1 mark)
Finding projection of b on a (1 mark)
Finding angle between the vectors (2 marks)
